input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Two separate issues:

**1. `StaticResource` doesn't update at runtime.**
`StaticResource` resolves once at XAML load and never re-evaluates. Switch to `DynamicResource`:

```xml
<!-- ❌ Won't update -->
<Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryText}" />

<!-- ✅ Updates when the dictionary changes -->
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryText}" />
```

Then swap the merged dictionary at runtime:

```csharp
var mergedDicts = Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
mergedDicts.Clear();
mergedDicts.Add(useDark ? new DarkTheme() : new LightTheme());
```

**2. Activity restart on system dark-mode toggle.**
Android destroys and recreates the Activity on `uiMode` changes. In your `MainActivity.cs`, add `UiMode` to `ConfigurationChanges`:

```csharp
[Activity(
    Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme",
    ConfigurationChanges = ConfigChanges.ScreenSize
                         | ConfigChanges.Orientation
                         | ConfigChanges.UiMode   // ← prevents restart
                         | ConfigChanges.ScreenLayout
                         | ConfigChanges.SmallestScreenSize)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

Then detect the change and swap your theme:

```csharp
public override void OnConfigurationChanged(Configuration newConfig)
{
    base.OnConfigurationChanged(newConfig);

    var isDark = (newConfig.UiMode & UiMode.NightMask) == UiMode.NightYes;
    // swap your ResourceDictionary here
}
```

**TL;DR:** `StaticResource` → `DynamicResource` for anything that changes at runtime, and add `ConfigChanges.UiMode` to prevent the activity restart.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about two issues with dark mode in their .NET MAUI Android app:

1. **StaticResource doesn't update at runtime** — This is a common mistake. `StaticResource` is resolved once at load time and never updates. They need to use `DynamicResource` instead for theme values that change at runtime.

2. **App restarts when Android dark mode is toggled** — By default, Android recreates the Activity when the UI mode (dark/light) changes. In .NET MAUI, you need to handle the `configChanges` attribute in the Android manifest to prevent the activity from being destroyed and recreated.


I should walk through the specific fixes they need to implement for both issues.
